_provenance.note: This Stage 1 JSON was reconstructed from the currently-published cards so external Stage 2 (Gemini bias) and Stage 3 (Codex fact-check) reviews can verify what readers see. It is not the original analysis — the original pipeline was not run on this issue.

headline: Tamil Schools Receive 2.1% of Education Budget for 5.4% of Students
context: Federal education budget analysis shows Tamil schools (SJKT) receive 2.1% of total allocation while serving 5.4% of the student population, the largest per-student deficit of any school stream.

cards.text: Tamil schools serve 5.4% of students and receive 2.1% of funding.
cards.sub: The 3.3-point gap translates to approximately RM480M in annual underfunding.

cards.h: 42% of SJKT buildings are classified as 'dilapidated' by MOE.
cards.s: The equivalent figure for national schools is 12%. For Chinese schools, 18%.
cards.d: 42% of SJKT buildings are classified as 'dilapidated' by MOE. The equivalent figure for national schools is 12%. For Chinese schools, 18%.

cards.h: Teacher vacancy rate in SJKT is 22% versus 8% nationally.
cards.s: Rural SJKT in estates have the highest vacancy rates, up to 34%.
cards.d: Teacher vacancy rate in SJKT is 22% versus 8% nationally. Rural SJKT in estates have the highest vacancy rates, up to 34%.

cards.h: Indian Malaysians have a poverty rate of 5.5%, third highest after Orang Asli and Sabah natives.
cards.s: Educational underfunding compounds existing economic disadvantage.
cards.d: Indian Malaysians have a poverty rate of 5.5%, third highest after Orang Asli and Sabah natives. Educational underfunding compounds existing economic disadvantage.

cards.text: The smallest stream gets the least money per student. Equity is the opposite of this.
Like giving the smallest classroom the least funding per student and then blaming the school for underperformance
cards.sub: Disproportionate under-funding creates the very performance gap that is then used to question the system's viability

cards.text: Tamil school underfunding is the clearest case of discriminatory allocation in Malaysian education. The gap is measurable, the protection clear, the remedy arithmetic.

confidence: Low — derived from published cards, original brief not on disk; submit for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
